# model_selection.validation.backtesting_forecaster_one_step { #spotforecast2_safe.model_selection.validation.backtesting_forecaster_one_step }

```python
model_selection.validation.backtesting_forecaster_one_step(
    forecaster,
    y,
    cv,
    metric,
    exog=None,
    interval=None,
    interval_method='bootstrapping',
    n_boot=250,
    use_in_sample_residuals=True,
    use_binned_residuals=True,
    random_state=123,
    return_predictors=False,
    n_jobs='auto',
    verbose=False,
    show_progress=True,
    suppress_warnings=False,
)
```

Backtesting of forecaster model using one-step-ahead predictions.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                    | Type                                                                                                                                           | Description                                                                       | Default           |
|-------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
| forecaster              | ([ForecasterRecursive](`ForecasterRecursive`), [ForecasterDirect](`ForecasterDirect`), [ForecasterEquivalentDate](`ForecasterEquivalentDate`)) | Forecaster model.                                                                 | _required_        |
| y                       | [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`)                                                                                                       | Training time series.                                                             | _required_        |
| cv                      | [OneStepAheadFold](`spotforecast2_safe.model_selection.split_one_step.OneStepAheadFold`)                                                       | OneStepAheadFold object with the information needed to split the data into folds. | _required_        |
| metric                  | [str](`str`) \| [Callable](`typing.Callable`) \| [list](`list`)                                                                                | Metric used to quantify the goodness of fit of the model.                         | _required_        |
| exog                    | [pd](`pandas`).[Series](`pandas.Series`) \| [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)                                                     | Exogenous variable/s included as predictor/s. Defaults to None.                   | `None`            |
| interval                | [float](`float`) \| [list](`list`) \| [tuple](`tuple`) \| [str](`str`) \| [object](`object`)                                                   | Specifies whether probabilistic predictions should be estimated.                  | `None`            |
| interval_method         | [str](`str`)                                                                                                                                   | Technique used to estimate prediction intervals.                                  | `'bootstrapping'` |
| n_boot                  | [int](`int`)                                                                                                                                   | Number of bootstrapping iterations.                                               | `250`             |
| use_in_sample_residuals | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Use residuals from training data.                                                 | `True`            |
| use_binned_residuals    | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Use binned residuals for intervals.                                               | `True`            |
| random_state            | [int](`int`)                                                                                                                                   | Seed for reproducibility.                                                         | `123`             |
| return_predictors       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Return predictors used for each prediction.                                       | `False`           |
| n_jobs                  | [int](`int`) \| [str](`str`)                                                                                                                   | Number of jobs to run in parallel.                                                | `'auto'`          |
| verbose                 | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Print information about the process.                                              | `False`           |
| show_progress           | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Whether to show a progress bar.                                                   | `True`            |
| suppress_warnings       | [bool](`bool`)                                                                                                                                 | Suppress spotforecast warnings.                                                   | `False`           |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                                                             | Description                                                                               |
|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| tuple  | ([pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`), [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)) | - metric_values: Value(s) of the metric(s). - backtest_predictions: Value of predictions. |

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

This function is designed for one-step-ahead backtesting,
where predictions are made for the next time step using the most recent data. The function handles the fitting and
prediction process for each fold defined in the `OneStepAheadFold` object,
and calculates the specified metric(s) based on the true and predicted values.
Depending on the `interval` and `interval_method` parameters, it can also generate probabilistic predictions.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.forecaster import ForecasterRecursive
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.model_selection.split_one_step import OneStepAheadFold
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.model_selection.validation import backtesting_forecaster_one_step
>>> # Create a forecaster and a one-step-ahead fold
>>> import numpy as np
>>> import pandas as pd
>>> from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
>>> y = pd.Series(np.random.randn(100), name='y')
>>> forecaster = ForecasterRecursive(estimator=LinearRegression(), lags=5)
>>> cv = OneStepAheadFold(initial_train_size=20, window_size=5)
>>> # Perform backtesting
>>> metric_values, backtest_predictions = backtesting_forecaster_one_step(
...     forecaster=forecaster,
...     y=y,
...     cv=cv,
...     metric='mean_squared_error',
...     exog=None,
...     interval=0.95,
...     interval_method='bootstrapping',
...     n_boot=20,
...     use_in_sample_residuals=True,
...     use_binned_residuals=False,
...     random_state=42,
...     return_predictors=False,
...     n_jobs=1,
...     verbose=True,
...     show_progress=True,
...     suppress_warnings=False
... )
# Note: For reliable bootstrapping with binned residuals, use a sufficiently large series and value spread.
# For random data, use_binned_residuals=False.
# TODO: Setting return_predictors=True requires ForecasterRecursive.create_predict_X().
```
